rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Ensure all identifiers used in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope
Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Write output dependency guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for the properties
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
